content:
🚀 awesome-osint-arsenal | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a centralized, easy-to-install, and organized collection of cybersecurity and OSINT tools for security professionals, researchers, and ethical hackers.
✨ Key Features: • 751+ OSINT & Security Tools • 50+ Categories • One-command installer (install.sh) • Categorized installation scripts (osint.sh, redteam.sh, etc.)
📖 Summary: Awesome OSINT Arsenal is a comprehensive shell script toolkit designed for Kali Linux and other Debian-based systems, offering over 750 tools categorized for various cybersecurity tasks. It simplifies the installation and access to tools for reconnaissance, red teaming, blue teaming, digital forensics, hardware analysis, and practice labs, with a special subset for Termux on Android.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 kadr | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: AI-assisted video editing with real-time GPU acceleration and integrated AI coding for motion graphics.
✨ Key Features: • Full multi-track editing (video/audio/text) • GPU compositing (WebGL2) for real-time preview and rendering • Keyframing for various properties (position, scale, rotation, opacity, masks) • True 3D capabilities for clips and tracks • Animated masks with multiple shapes and feathering
📖 Summary: Kadr is an AI-native, GPU-accelerated video editor built with Electron, React, and TypeScript. It integrates AI agents directly into the timeline, allowing for real-time editing assistance and automated features like subtitle generation and motion graphics creation.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 homer | Vue
🎯 Primary Use Case: A self-hosted, customizable dashboard for organizing and quickly accessing frequently used web services and applications.
✨ Key Features: • Lightweight & Fast • Low / No maintenance • Simple YAML file configuration • Installable (PWA) • Smart cards
📖 Summary: Homer is a lightweight, self-hosted static homepage designed to provide a simple and fast dashboard for managing your server's services. It is configured via a YAML file and offers features like smart cards, fuzzy search, and theme customization.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 penpot | Clojure
🎯 Primary Use Case: Collaborative design and development of digital products, bridging the gap between designers and developers with open standards and self-hosting capabilities.
✨ Key Features: • Open-source design and prototyping platform • Real-time collaboration • Self-hosting for full infrastructure ownership • Works with open standards (SVG, CSS, HTML, JSON)
📖 Summary: Penpot is an open-source design and prototyping platform that enables teams to collaborate on digital product development. It emphasizes full ownership of design infrastructure through self-hosting and works with open standards like SVG, CSS, and HTML, integrating design and code with features like Design Tokens and a powerful API.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
